title: DiffGate claim file
description: Structured input for `diffgate verify --claim-file <file>` (pass `-` to read this JSON from stdin). It gives the CLI the same structured `claimed_actions` payload the MCP `verify_edit` tool already accepts, so an agent emitting JSON no longer has to round-trip through the `--claim` mini-DSL. Two shapes are accepted: a SINGLE-file claim (used with --before/--after on the command line) and a MULTI-file claim that carries its own before/after file paths so a whole edit spanning many files is gated in one call.

oneOf.title: Single-file claim
oneOf.description: A bare list of actions, or an object wrapping one. Use with `--before X --after X.new --claim-file claims.json`.

oneOf.title: Multi-file claim
oneOf.description: Gate an edit spanning several files in one call. Each entry names its own before/after file and the actions claimed for that file. Mismatches from every file aggregate into a single Verdict; the failing file's path is attached to each mismatch reason.

base_dir.description: Optional directory the per-file `before`/`after` paths are resolved against. Defaults to the claim file's own directory (or the cwd for stdin).

path.description: Logical path used to label this file in mismatch reasons. Defaults to the `after` path.

before.description: Path to the file BEFORE the edit (relative to base_dir, or absolute).

after.description: Path to the file AFTER the edit (relative to base_dir, or absolute).

language.description: Override the language auto-detected from the file extension.

kind.description: What the agent claims it did to `symbol`.

symbol.description: Source name for rename/move; the affected name for add/delete/signature_change. The shorthand 'old->new' (or 'old→new') is also accepted for rename/move.

new_symbol.description: Target name for a rename (or destination symbol for a move). Optional when `symbol` uses the 'old->new' shorthand.

scope.description: Containing class/module the symbol lives in (e.g. 'MyClass'). Empty means module level / wildcard. For `move`, this is the destination scope the symbol must land in.
